title: wasm: NieRelayClient::connect() has no timeout on challenge_rx.await — hangs forever on misbehaving relay
description: wasm/src/client.rs NieRelayClient::connect() installs a notify callback to capture the relay's 'challenge' notification, then awaits challenge_rx with no timeout (line 77-79). If the relay is misbehaving (slow, incorrect, or sends a non-challenge first message), the browser Promise returned by NieClient::connect() never resolves or rejects — it hangs indefinitely. In contrast, Tokio-based clients (FFI, CLI) can be wrapped with tokio::time::timeout. The WebAssembly single-threaded executor has no built-in cancellation mechanism; without a gloo_timers or js-based timeout, the connect Promise is permanently pending. Impact: any WASM caller awaiting connect() on a slow/broken relay will hang indefinitely with no error, no timeout, and no way for JS to detect or recover.
